title: kadua littoralis
intro: Kadua littoralis, also known as the Hawaiian wilelaiki, is a flowering plant species native to Hawaii and other Pacific Island nations. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating skin conditions, due to its high concentration of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. Kadua littoralis is often prepared as a tea or tincture for medicinal use, and its leaves can also be made into a poultice or infused into oils for topical application.
